diagrams.title: Autoencoder Architecture Overview
diagrams.description: Shows the complete autoencoder structure with encoder, bottleneck (latent space), and decoder. The input data flows through the encoder to compress it into a lower-dimensional representation in the bottleneck, then flows through the decoder to reconstruct the output. This helps learners understand the fundamental compression-reconstruction paradigm.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the basic architecture and data flow of autoencoders, showing how dimension reduction works through the bottleneck

diagrams.title: Training Process and Loss Function
diagrams.description: Illustrates the training loop where the autoencoder learns to minimize reconstruction error. Shows the input being encoded and decoded, then compared with the original input to compute loss. This visualizes the self-supervised learning nature of autoencoders.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how autoencoders learn by minimizing the difference between input and reconstructed output

diagrams.title: Latent Space Representation
diagrams.description: Shows the compressed representation in the bottleneck as a 2D space where different input samples map to different points. Demonstrates how similar inputs cluster together and how the latent space captures essential features. Useful for understanding feature learning and representation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how autoencoders learn meaningful latent representations and capture data similarities in compressed form
